content: Liver is rich in preformed vitamin A (retinol), which supports cellular repair, immune function, skin health, and hormone synthesis.
Retinol is readily available in liver and does not require enzymatic conversion, making it easily absorbed by the body.
Liver contains heme iron and vitamin B12 in therapeutic amounts, essential for red blood cell production, oxygen transport, energy metabolism, and mental clarity.
Liver is a dense source of folate, zinc, copper, selenium, and choline, crucial for fertility, detoxification, and nervous system health.
Liver consumption has led to dramatic improvements in skin, energy, mood, and hormones within weeks for many people dealing with various health issues.
Modern nutrition often overlooks the importance of traditional foods like liver, which have been integral to human diets for thousands of years.
A new meat stick product, combining 100% Australian grass-fed beef, real liver, real heart, and organic spices, delivers 64 grams of protein, zero carbs, and a full spectrum of micronutrients.
This product aims to make liver more accessible and palatable for those interested in incorporating it into their diet for improved health outcomes.
